songs.title: Mine
songs.lyrics:
Oh-oh, oh-oh
Oh-oh, oh-oh

You were in college working part time waitin' tables
Left a small town, never looked back
I was a flight risk with a fear of falling
Wonderin' why we bother with love if it never lasts

I say, "Can you believe it?"
As we're lyin' on the couch
The moment I could see it
Yes, yes, I can see it now

Do you remember, we were sittin' there by the water?
You put your arm around me for the first time
You made a rebel of a careless man's careful daughter
You are the best thing that's ever been mine

Flash forward and we're takin' on the world together
And there's a drawer of my things at your place
You learn my secrets and you figure out why I'm guarded
You say we'll never make my parents' mistakes

But we got bills to pay
We got nothing figured out
When it was hard to take
Yes, yes, this is what I thought about

Do you remember, we were sittin' there by the water?
You put your arm around me for the first time
You made a rebel of a careless man's careful daughter
You are the best thing that's ever been mine
Do you remember all the city lights on the water?
You saw me start to believe for the first time
You made a rebel of a careless man's careful daughter
You are the best thing that's ever been mine

Oh-oh, oh-oh

And I remember that fight, 2:30AM
As everything was slipping right out of our hands
I ran out crying and you followed me out into the street
Braced myself for the goodbye
'Cause that's all I've ever known
Then you took me by surprise
You said, "I'll never leave you alone"

You said, "I remember how we felt sitting by the water
And every time I look at you, it's like the first time
I fell in love with a careless man's careful daughter
She is the best thing that's ever been mine"

Hold on, make it last
Hold on, never turn back
(Hold on) You made a rebel of a careless man's careful daughter (Make it last)
(Hold on) You are the best thing that's ever been mine (Hold on)

Yes, yes, do you believe it? (Hold on)
Yes, yes, we're gonna make it now (Hold on)
Yes, yes, I can see it
Yes, yes, I can see it now

vocab.definition: a feeling of anxiety or apprehension about something
vocab.lyric_snippet: I was a flight risk with a fear of falling
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word 'fear' to describe her anxiety about falling in love or getting hurt.

vocab.definition: having a defensive or protective attitude, often due to past experiences
vocab.lyric_snippet: You learn my secrets and you figure out why I'm guarded
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word 'guarded' to describe her defensive nature, which is revealed to her partner.

vocab.definition: a person who rejects or challenges conventional rules or authority
vocab.lyric_snippet: You made a rebel of a careless man's careful daughter
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word 'rebel' to describe how her partner changed her and made her more confident.

vocab.definition: lacking attention to detail or a sense of responsibility
vocab.lyric_snippet: You made a rebel of a careless man's careful daughter
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word 'careless' to describe her past self, who was more reckless and irresponsible.

vocab.definition: to understand or work out something
vocab.lyric_snippet: You say we'll never make my parents' mistakes
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word 'figured' to describe how her partner understands and supports her.

vocab.definition: taking or seizing something
vocab.lyric_snippet: Flash forward and we're takin' on the world together
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word 'takin'' to describe her and her partner's adventurous spirit.

songs.title: Sparks Fly
songs.lyrics:
The way you move is like a full on rainstorm and I'm a house of cards
You're the kind of reckless that should send me runnin'
But I kinda know that I won't get far
And you stood there in front of me, just close enough to touch
Close enough to hope you couldn't see what I was thinkin' of

Drop everything now, meet me in the pouring rain
Kiss me on the sidewalk, take away the pain
'Cause I see sparks fly whenever you smile
Get me with those green eyes, baby, as the lights go down
Give me something that'll haunt me when you're not around
'Cause I see sparks fly whenever you smile

My mind forgets to remind me you're a bad idea
You touch me once and it's really somethin'
You find I'm even better than you imagined I would be
I'm on my guard for the rest of the world
But with you, I know it's no good
And I could wait patiently, but I really wish you would

Drop everything now, meet me in the pouring rain
Kiss me on the sidewalk, take away the pain
'Cause I see sparks fly whenever you smile
Get me with those green eyes, baby, as the lights go down
Give me something that'll haunt me when you're not around
'Cause I see sparks fly whenever you smile

I run my fingers through your hair and watch the lights go wild
Just keep on keepin' your eyes on me, it's just wrong enough to make it feel right
And lead me up the staircase, won't you whisper soft and slow?
I'm captivated by you, baby, like a firework show

Drop everything now, meet me in the pouring rain
Kiss me on the sidewalk (Ah-ah), take away the pain
'Cause I see sparks fly whenever you smile
Get me with those green eyes, baby, as the lights go down
Give me something that'll haunt me when you're not around
'Cause I see sparks fly whenever you smile

And the sparks fly
Oh, baby, smile
And the sparks fly

vocab.definition: acting or behaving in a way that is careless and irresponsible, often in a way that is exciting or thrilling.
vocab.lyric_snippet: You're the kind of reckless that should send me runnin'
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the kind of behavior that is exciting and thrilling, but also potentially hurtful or damaging.

vocab.definition: to continue to affect or disturb someone's thoughts or feelings, often in a way that is unpleasant or unwelcome.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Give me something that'll haunt me when you're not around
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the kind of emotional impact that someone can have on her, even when they are not present.

vocab.definition: completely and utterly fascinated or enchanted by something or someone.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I'm captivated by you, baby, like a firework show
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the way she feels when she is around the person she is singing about.

vocab.definition: completely out of control or unmanageable.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Just keep on keepin' your eyes on me, it's just wrong enough to make it feel right And watch the lights go wild
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the way the lights behave when she is with the person she is singing about.

vocab.definition: a type of explosive device that is designed to produce a bright display of light and color in the sky.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I'm captivated by you, baby, like a firework show
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word as a metaphor to describe the way she feels when she is with the person she is singing about.

songs.title: Back to December
songs.lyrics:
I'm so glad you made time to see me
How's life? Tell me, how's your family?
I haven't seen them in a while
You've been good, busier than ever
We small talk, work and the weather
Your guard is up and I know why

Because the last time you saw me
Is still burned in the back of your mind
You gave me roses and I left them there to die

So this is me swallowing my pride
Standin' in front of you sayin', "I'm sorry for that night"
And I go back to December all the time
It turns out freedom ain't nothing but missin' you
Wishin' I'd realized what I had when you were mine
I go back to December, turn around and make it alright
I go back to December all the time

These days, I haven't been sleeping
Stayin' up, playing back myself leavin'
When your birthday passed and I didn't call
Then I think about summer, all the beautiful times
I watched you laughing from the passenger side
And realized I loved you in the fall

And then the cold came, the dark days
When fear crept into my mind
You gave me all your love and all I gave you was goodbye

So this is me swallowing my pride
Standin' in front of you, sayin', "I'm sorry for that night"
And I go back to December all the time
It turns out freedom ain't nothing but missin' you
Wishin' I'd realized what I had when you were mine
I go back to December, turn around and change my own mind
I go back to December all the time

I miss your tanned skin, your sweet smile
So good to me, so right
And how you held me in your arms that September night
The first time you ever saw me cry
Maybe this is wishful thinkin'
Probably mindless dreaming
But if we loved again, I swear I'd love you right
I'd go back in time and change it, but I can't
So, if the chain is on your door, I understand

But this is me swallowing my pride
Standin' in front of you, sayin', "I'm sorry for that night"
And I go back to December
It turns out freedom ain't nothing but missin' you
Wishin' I'd realized what I had when you were mine
I go back to December, turn around and make it alright
I go back to December, turn around and change my own mind
I go back to December all the time

All the time

vocab.definition: to suppress or overcome one's pride or emotions
vocab.lyric_snippet: So this is me swallowing my pride
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her act of apologizing and taking responsibility for her past mistakes.

vocab.definition: a mental or emotional defense mechanism
vocab.lyric_snippet: Your guard is up and I know why
vocab.context: Taylor notes that the person's emotional guard is up, indicating that they are defensive or protective due to past experiences.

vocab.definition: to be deeply affected or traumatized by a memory
vocab.lyric_snippet: Is still burned in the back of your mind
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe how the person's memory of their past encounter with her is still painful and traumatic.

vocab.definition: to become aware of or understand something
vocab.lyric_snippet: Wishin' I'd realized what I had when you were mine
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to express regret for not appreciating the person when they were in a relationship.

vocab.definition: a colloquial or informal way of saying 'missing'
vocab.lyric_snippet: It turns out freedom ain't nothing but missin' you
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the feeling of missing someone, highlighting the idea that freedom without the person is not as fulfilling.

vocab.definition: having or expressing a hopeful or optimistic attitude
vocab.lyric_snippet: Maybe this is wishful thinkin'
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her hopes and dreams of reuniting with the person.

vocab.definition: lacking thought or consideration
vocab.lyric_snippet: Probably mindless dreaming
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her daydreaming or fantasizing about the person.

vocab.definition: having a sun-kissed or golden complexion
vocab.lyric_snippet: I miss your tanned skin
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the person's physical appearance, highlighting their attractive features.

songs.title: Speak Now
songs.lyrics:
I am not the kind of girl
Who should be rudely bargin' in on a white veil occasion
But you are not the kind of boy
Who should be marrying the wrong girl
I sneak in and see your friends
And her snotty little family all dressed in pastel
And she is yelling at a bridesmaid
Somewhere back inside a room
Wearin' a gown shaped like a pastry

This is surely not what you thought it would be
I lose myself in a daydream
Where I stand and say

Don't say yes, run away now
I'll meet you when you're out
Of the church, at the back door
Don't wait or say a single vow
You need to hear me out
And they said, "Speak now"

Fond gestures are exchanged
And the organ starts to play a song that sounds like a death march
And I am hiding in the curtains
It seems that I was uninvited by your lovely bride-to-be

She floats down the aisle like a pageant queen
But I know you wish it was me
You wish it was me, don't you?

Don't say yes, run away now
I'll meet you when you're out
Of the church, at the back door
Don't wait or say a single vow
You need to hear me out
And they said, "Speak now"
Don't say yes, run away now (Run away now)
I'll meet you when you're out
Of the church, at the back door
Don't wait or say a single vow (Say a single vow)
Your time is running out
And they said, "Speak now"

Ooh (Run away now), la
Ooh (Say a single vow), ooh

I hear the preacher say
"Speak now or forever hold your peace"
There's the silence, there's my last chance
I stand up with shaky hands, all eyes on me
Horrified looks from everyone in the room
But I'm only lookin' at you

I am not the kind of girl
Who should be rudely bargin' in on a white veil occasion
But you are not the kind of boy
Who should be marrying the wrong girl, haha

So, don't say yes, run away now
I'll meet you when you're out
Of the church, at the back door
Don't wait or say a single vow
You need to hear me out
They said, "Speak now"
And you say, "Let's run away now (Run away now)
I'll meet you when I'm out
Of my tux, at the back door
Baby, I didn't say my vows (Say a single vow)
So glad you were around
When they said, 'Speak now'"

vocab.definition: in a rough or impolite manner
vocab.lyric_snippet: Who should be rudely bargin' in on a white veil occasion
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'rudely' to describe her sudden interruption of the wedding, highlighting her impolite and unexpected behavior.

vocab.definition: to enter or approach someone or something in a forceful or aggressive manner
vocab.lyric_snippet: Who should be rudely bargin' in on a white veil occasion
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'bargin'' to describe her sudden and forceful entry into the wedding, emphasizing her bold and unexpected actions.

vocab.definition: a pale or delicate color, especially one used in painting or dyeing
vocab.lyric_snippet: And her snotty little family all dressed in pastel
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'pastel' to describe the bride's family's attire, highlighting their delicate and pale appearance.

vocab.definition: having a haughty or condescending attitude
vocab.lyric_snippet: And her snotty little family all dressed in pastel
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'snotty' to describe the bride's family, emphasizing their haughty and condescending attitude.

vocab.definition: a long, loose-fitting dress, especially one worn for formal occasions
vocab.lyric_snippet: Wearin' a gown shaped like a pastry
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'gown' to describe the bridesmaid's attire, highlighting its formal and elegant appearance.

vocab.definition: a baked food, especially one made with pastry dough
vocab.lyric_snippet: Wearin' a gown shaped like a pastry
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'pastry' to describe the bridesmaid's gown, emphasizing its unusual and whimsical design.

vocab.definition: a public display or exhibition, especially one of a formal or ceremonial nature
vocab.lyric_snippet: She floats down the aisle like a pageant queen
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'pageant' to describe the bride's behavior, highlighting her formal and ceremonial appearance.

vocab.definition: a person who preaches or delivers sermons, especially in a church
vocab.lyric_snippet: I hear the preacher say
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'preacher' to describe the person officiating the wedding, emphasizing their role in the ceremony.

vocab.definition: for an indefinitely long time
vocab.lyric_snippet: Speak now or forever hold your peace
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'forever' to describe the consequences of not speaking up, emphasizing the long-term impact of her decision.

vocab.definition: feeling shocked or frightened
vocab.lyric_snippet: Horrified looks from everyone in the room
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'horrified' to describe the reactions of the people in the room, emphasizing their shock and fright.

songs.title: Dear John
songs.lyrics:
Long were the nights when
My days once revolved around you
Counting my footsteps
Praying the floor won't fall through again
And my mother accused me of losing my mind
But I swore I was fine
You paint me a blue sky
Then go back and turn it to rain
And I lived in your chess game
But you changed the rules everyday
Wondering which version of you I might get on the phone tonight
Well, I stopped pickin' up and this song is to let you know why

Dear John, I see it all now that you're gone
Don't you think I was too young to be messed with?
The girl in the dress cried the whole way home
I should've known

Well, maybe it's me
And my blind optimism to blame
Or maybe it's you and your sick need
To give love and take it away
And you'll add my name to your long list of traitors
Who don't understand
And I look back in regret how I ignored when they said
"Run as fast as you can"

Dear John, I see it all now that you're gone
Don't you think I was too young to be messed with?
The girl in the dress cried the whole way home
Dear John, I see it all now, it was wrong
Don't you think nineteen's too young
To be played by your dark, twisted games when I loved you so?
I should've known

You are an expert at sorry and keeping lines blurry
Never impressed by me acing your tests
All the girls that you've run dry have tired lifeless eyes
'Cause you burned them out
But I took your matches before fire could catch me
So don't look now
I'm shining like fireworks over your sad empty town
Oh, oh

Dear John, I see it all now that you're gone
Don't you think I was too young to be messed with?
The girl in the dress cried the whole way home
I see it all now that you're gone
Don't you think I was too young to be messed with?
The girl in the dress wrote you a song
You should've known

You should've known
Don't you think I was too young?
You should've known

vocab.definition: to rotate or turn around a central point or axis
vocab.lyric_snippet: My days once revolved around you
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe how her life was centered around John.

vocab.definition: to formally charge someone with a crime or wrongdoing
vocab.lyric_snippet: My mother accused me of losing my mind
vocab.context: Taylor's mother accused her of being unstable due to her relationship with John.

vocab.definition: to confuse or disturb someone's mind or emotions
vocab.lyric_snippet: Don't you think I was too young to be messed with?
vocab.context: Taylor accuses John of confusing and manipulating her.

vocab.definition: a feeling or attitude of hopefulness about the future
vocab.lyric_snippet: My blind optimism to blame
vocab.context: Taylor reflects on her overly optimistic attitude towards John.

vocab.definition: people who betray the trust or loyalty of others
vocab.lyric_snippet: You'll add my name to your long list of traitors
vocab.context: Taylor accuses John of being a manipulator who uses people and discards them.

vocab.definition: a feeling of sadness or disappointment about something that has happened
vocab.lyric_snippet: I look back in regret how I ignored when they said
vocab.context: Taylor reflects on her regret for not listening to others' warnings about John.

vocab.definition: bent or distorted in an unnatural way
vocab.lyric_snippet: Your dark, twisted games
vocab.context: Taylor describes John's manipulative behavior as twisted and unnatural.

vocab.definition: not clear or distinct
vocab.lyric_snippet: You are an expert at sorry and keeping lines blurry
vocab.context: Taylor accuses John of being evasive and unclear in his relationships.

vocab.definition: not feeling or showing fatigue
vocab.lyric_snippet: All the girls that you've run dry have tired lifeless eyes
vocab.context: Taylor describes the girls John has used as having lifeless eyes, implying they are exhausted.

vocab.definition: to grasp or seize something quickly
vocab.lyric_snippet: So don't look now I'm shining like fireworks over your sad empty town before fire could catch me
vocab.context: Taylor describes herself as being able to avoid getting hurt by John's manipulation.

vocab.definition: giving off light, often in a bright or radiant way
vocab.lyric_snippet: So don't look now I'm shining like fireworks over your sad empty town
vocab.context: Taylor describes herself as being happy and radiant after escaping John's manipulation.

songs.title: Mean
songs.lyrics:
You, with your words like knives
And swords and weapons that you use against me
You have knocked me off my feet again
Got me feeling like I'm nothing
You, with your voice like nails on a chalkboard
Calling me out when I'm wounded
You, pickin' on the weaker man

Well, you can take me down
With just one single blow
But you don't know what you don't know

Someday, I'll be livin' in a big ole city
And all you're ever gonna be is mean
Someday, I'll be big enough so you can't hit me
And all you're ever gonna be is mean
Why you gotta be so mean?

You, with your switching sides
And your wildfire lies and your humiliation
You have pointed out my flaws again
As if I don't already see them
I walk with my head down, trying to block you out
'Cause I'll never impress you
I just wanna feel okay again

I bet you got pushed around
Somebody made you cold
But the cycle ends right now
'Cause you can't lead me down that road
And you don't know what you don't know

Someday, I'll be livin' in a big ole city
And all you're ever gonna be is mean
Someday, I'll be big enough so you can't hit me
And all you're ever gonna be is mean
Why you gotta be so mean?

And I can see you years from now in a bar
Talking over a football game
With that same big, loud opinion
But nobody's listening
Washed up and ranting about the same old bitter things
Drunk and grumblin' on about how I can't sing
But all you are is mean

All you are is mean
And a liar, and pathetic
And alone in life, and mean
And mean, and mean, and mean

But someday, I'll be livin' in a big ole city
And all you're ever gonna be is mean
Yeah, someday, I'll be big enough so you can't hit me
And all you're ever gonna be is mean
Why you gotta be so mean?
Someday, I'll be livin' in a big ole city
(Why you gotta be so mean?)
And all you're ever gonna be is mean
(Why you gotta be so mean?)
Someday, I'll be big enough so you can't hit me
(Why you gotta be so mean?)
And all you're ever gonna be is mean
Why you gotta be so mean?

vocab.definition: sharp, pointed objects used for cutting or stabbing
vocab.lyric_snippet: You, with your words like knives
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'knives' to describe the hurtful and cutting effect of the person's words.

vocab.definition: long, sharp-edged weapons used for fighting
vocab.lyric_snippet: And swords and weapons that you use against me
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'swords' to emphasize the aggressive and violent nature of the person's words.

vocab.definition: hurt or injured, either physically or emotionally
vocab.lyric_snippet: Calling me out when I'm wounded
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'wounded' to describe her emotional state, making her more vulnerable to the person's attacks.

vocab.definition: having less strength or power
vocab.lyric_snippet: You, pickin' on the weaker man
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'weaker' to describe herself, implying that the person is taking advantage of her vulnerability.

vocab.definition: a rapidly spreading and uncontrollable fire
vocab.lyric_snippet: And your wildfire lies and your humiliation
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'wildfire' to describe the person's lies, implying that they are spreading quickly and causing damage.

vocab.definition: a feeling of shame or embarrassment, often caused by being treated unfairly or being made to look foolish
vocab.lyric_snippet: And your wildfire lies and your humiliation
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'humiliation' to describe the person's actions, implying that they are causing her to feel ashamed and embarrassed.

vocab.definition: having a strong feeling of resentment or anger, often caused by a past hurt or injustice
vocab.lyric_snippet: Drunk and grumblin' on about the same old bitter things
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'bitter' to describe the person's attitude, implying that they are still holding onto past resentments.

vocab.definition: speaking or writing in a loud and angry manner, often about a particular topic
vocab.lyric_snippet: Talking over a football game / With that same big, loud opinion / But nobody's listening / Washed up and ranting about the same old bitter things
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'ranting' to describe the person's behavior, implying that they are speaking loudly and angrily about their grievances.

vocab.definition: feeling or showing pity, often because of being weak or helpless
vocab.lyric_snippet: And a liar, and pathetic / And alone in life, and mean
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'pathetic' to describe the person, implying that they are weak and helpless.

vocab.definition: speaking in a low, discontented tone, often about a particular topic
vocab.lyric_snippet: Drunk and grumblin' on about the same old bitter things
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'grumblin'' to describe the person's behavior, implying that they are speaking in a low, discontented tone about their grievances.

songs.title: The Story of Us
songs.lyrics:
I used to think, one day, we'd tell the story of us
How we met and the sparks flew instantly
And people would say, "They're the lucky ones"
I used to know my place was the spot next to you
Now, I'm searching the room for an empty seat
'Cause lately, I don't even know what page you're on

Oh, a simple complication
Miscommunications lead to fallout
So many things that I wish you knew
So many walls up, I can't break through

Now, I'm standin' alone in a crowded room
And we're not speakin'
And I'm dyin' to know
Is it killin' you like it's killin' me? Yeah
I don't know what to say since the twist of fate
When it all broke down
And the story of us looks a lot like a tragedy now

Next chapter

How'd we end up this way?
See me nervously pulling at my clothes and trying to look busy
And you're doin' your best to avoid me
I'm starting to think, one day, I'll tell the story of us
How I was losing my mind when I saw you here
But you held your pride like you should've held me

Oh, I'm scared to see the ending
Why are we pretending this is nothin'?
I'd tell you I miss you, but I don't know how
I've never heard silence quite this loud

Now, I'm standin' alone in a crowded room
And we're not speakin'
And I'm dyin' to know
Is it killin' you like it's killin' me? Yeah
I don't know what to say since the twist of fate
When it all broke down
And the story of us looks a lot like a tragedy now

This is lookin' like a contest
Of who can act like they care less
But I liked it better when you were on my side
The battle's in your hands now
But I would lay my armor down
If you said you'd rather love than fight

So many things that you wish I knew
But the story of us might be ending soon

Now, I'm standin' alone in a crowded room
And we're not speakin'
And I'm dyin' to know
Is it killin' you like it's killin' me? Yeah
And I don't know what to say since the twist of fate
When it all broke down
And the story of us looks a lot like a tragedy now, now, now
And we're not speakin'
And I'm dyin' to know
Is it killin' you like it's killin' me? Yeah
I don't know what to say since the twist of fate
'Cause we're going down
And the story of us looks a lot like a tragedy now

The end

vocab.definition: A problem or situation that is difficult to deal with or resolve.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Oh, a simple complication
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the miscommunications that led to the breakdown of their relationship.

vocab.definition: The act of communicating something incorrectly or unclearly.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Miscommunications lead to fallout
vocab.context: Taylor highlights the role of miscommunications in the downfall of their relationship.

vocab.definition: An unexpected turn of events that changes the course of something.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I don't know what to say since the twist of fate
vocab.context: Taylor uses this phrase to describe the unexpected events that led to the breakdown of their relationship.

vocab.definition: A sad or unfortunate event, especially one that is caused by a series of unfortunate circumstances.
vocab.lyric_snippet: And the story of us looks a lot like a tragedy now
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the state of their relationship, which has become sad and unfortunate.

vocab.definition: Feeling or showing anxiety or apprehension.
vocab.lyric_snippet: See me nervously pulling at my clothes
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her own behavior, showing how she is feeling anxious and apprehensive.

vocab.definition: Acting as if something is true or real when it is not.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Why are we pretending this is nothin'?
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the way she and her partner are trying to hide their true feelings.

vocab.definition: The state of being quiet or still, especially because of a lack of sound or activity.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I've never heard silence quite this loud
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the uncomfortable and oppressive silence between her and her partner.

vocab.definition: A competition or contest, especially one in which people or things are compared or judged.
vocab.lyric_snippet: This is lookin' like a contest
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the way she and her partner are trying to outdo each other in a game of who can act like they care less.

vocab.definition: A protective covering, especially one made of metal or other hard material.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I would lay my armor down
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the emotional protection she has built up around herself.

songs.title: Never Grow Up
songs.lyrics:
Your little hand's wrapped around my finger
And it's so quiet in the world tonight
Your little eyelids flutter 'cause you're dreaming
So I tuck you in, turn on your favourite nightlight

To you, everything's funny
You got nothing to regret
I'd give all I have, honey
If you could stay like that

Oh, darling, don't you ever grow up
Don't you ever grow up, just stay this little
Oh, darling, don't you ever grow up
Don't you ever grow up, it could stay this simple
I won't let nobody hurt you
Won't let no one break your heart
And no one will desert you
Just try to never grow up

Never grow up

You're in the car, on the way to the movies
And you're mortified your mom's dropping you off
At fourteen, there's just so much you can't do
And you can't wait to move out someday and call your own shots

But don't make her drop you off around the block
Remember that she's gettin' older, too
And don't lose the way that you dance around
In your PJs getting ready for school

Oh, darling, don't you ever grow up
Don't you ever grow up, just stay this little
Oh, darling, don't you ever grow up
Don't you ever grow up, it could stay this simple
No one's ever burned you
Nothing's ever left you scarred
And even though you want to
Just try to never grow up

Take pictures in your mind of your childhood room
Memorize what it sounded like when your dad gets home
Remember the footsteps, remember the words said
And all your little brother's favorite songs
I just realized everything I have is, someday, gonna be gone

So, here I am in my new apartment
In a big city, they just dropped me off
It's so much colder than I thought it would be
So I tuck myself in and turn my nightlight on

Wish I'd never grown up
I wish I'd never grown up
Oh, I don't wanna grow up
Wish I'd never grown up, I could still be little
Oh, I don't wanna grow up
Wish I'd never grown up, it could still be simple
Oh, darling, don't you ever grow up
Don't you ever grow up, just stay this little
Oh, darlin', don't you ever grow up
Don't you ever grow up, it could stay this simple
I won't let nobody hurt you (Never grow up)
Won't let no one break your heart
And even though you want to
Please, try to never grow up

Oh, oh (Never grow up)
Don't you ever grow up
Oh (Never grow up)
Just never grow up

vocab.definition: extremely embarrassed or humiliated
vocab.lyric_snippet: And you're mortified your mom's dropping you off
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to convey the embarrassment and self-consciousness of a 14-year-old girl being dropped off at the movies by her mom.

vocab.definition: to abandon or leave someone in a difficult or unpleasant situation

vocab.definition: made a particular noise

vocab.definition: to understand or become aware of something

vocab.definition: to leave someone or something in a particular place

vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the feeling of being abandoned or left behind.

vocab.definition: to learn or remember something by heart
vocab.lyric_snippet: Memorize what it sounded like when your dad gets home
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the act of remembering and holding onto memories.

vocab.definition: feeling or expressing a sentimental longing or wistful affection for the past

songs.title: Enchanted
songs.lyrics:
There I was again tonight
Forcing laughter, faking smiles
Same old tired, lonely place
Walls of insincerity
Shifting eyes and vacancy
Vanished when I saw your face
All I can say is it was enchanting to meet you

Your eyes whispered, "Have we met?"
Across the room, your silhouette
Starts to make its way to me
The playful conversation starts
Counter all your quick remarks
Like passing notes in secrecy
And it was enchanting to meet you
All I can say is I was enchanted to meet you

This night is sparklin', don't you let it go
I'm wonderstruck, blushin' all the way home
I'll spend forever wonderin' if you knew
I was enchanted to meet you

The lingering question kept me up
2 a.m., who do you love?
I wonder 'til I'm wide awake
And now, I'm pacing back and forth
Wishing you were at my door
I'd open up and you would say
"Hey, it was enchanting to meet you"
All I know is I was enchanted to meet you

This night is sparklin', don't you let it go
I'm wonderstruck, blushin' all the way home
I'll spend forever wonderin' if you knew
This night is flawless, don't you let it go
I'm wonderstruck, dancing around all alone
I'll spend forever wonderin' if you knew
I was enchanted to meet you

This is me praying that
This was the very first page
Not where the storyline ends
My thoughts will echo your name
Until I see you again
These are the words I held back
As I was leaving too soon
I was enchanted to meet you
Please, don't be in love with someone else
Please, don't have somebody waiting on you
Please, don't be in love with someone else (Ooh)
Please, don't have somebody waiting on you (Ooh, oh)

This night is sparklin', don't you let it go
I'm wonderstruck, blushin' all the way home
I'll spend forever wonderin' if you knew
This night is flawless (Please, don't be in love with someone else)
Don't you let it go
I'm wonderstruck (Please, don't have somebody waiting on you)
Dancing around all alone
I'll spend forever (Please, don't be in love with someone else)
Wonderin' if you knew
I was enchanted to meet you

Please, don't be in love with someone else
Please, don't have somebody waiting on you

vocab.definition: A lack of sincerity or genuineness in one's words or actions.

vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the atmosphere of the party, where people are pretending to be happy and friendly.

vocab.definition: A feeling of emptiness or a lack of interest.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Shifting eyes and vacancy
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the emotional state of the people at the party, where they seem uninterested and disconnected.

vocab.definition: The outline or shape of a person or object against a lighter background.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Across the room, your silhouette
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the way the person's shape is visible from a distance, creating a sense of mystery and intrigue.

vocab.definition: To respond or oppose something, often in a clever or witty way.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Counter all your quick remarks
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her attempt to match the person's quick wit and clever responses.

vocab.definition: The state of being secret or hidden from others.

vocab.definition: To stay or remain in a place or situation for a longer time than intended.

vocab.definition: Perfect or without any flaws or imperfections.

vocab.definition: A repeated sound or a lingering memory.

songs.title: Better than Revenge
songs.lyrics:
Now, go stand in the corner and think about what you did (Ha)
Haha, time for a little revenge

The story starts when it was hot and it was summer and
I had it all, I had him right there where I wanted him
She came along, got him alone, and let's hear the applause
She took him faster than you can say "Sabotage"
I never saw it coming, wouldn't have suspected it
I underestimated just who I was dealing with
She had to know the pain was beating on me like a drum
She underestimated just who she was stealing from

She's not a saint and she's not what you think
She's an actress, woah
She's better known for the things that she does
On the mattress, woah
Soon, she's gonna find stealing other people's toys
On the playground won't make you many friends
She should keep in mind, she should keep in mind
There is nothing I do better than revenge, ha

She looks at life like it's a party and she's on the list
She looks at me like I'm a trend and she's so over it
I think her ever-present frown is a little troubling and

But sophistication isn't what you wear or who you know
Or pushing people down to get you where you wanna go
Oh, they didn't teach you that in prep school so it's up to me
But no amount of vintage dresses gives you dignity
(Think about what you did)

She's not a saint and she's not what you think
She's an actress, woah
She's better known for the things that she does
On the mattress, woah
Soon, she's gonna find stealing other people's toys
On the playground won't make you many friends
She should keep in mind, she should keep in mind
There is nothing I do better than revenge, ha

I'm just another thing for you to roll your eyes at, honey
You might have him, but haven't you heard?
I'm just another thing for you to roll your eyes at, honey
You might have him, but I always get the last word
Woah, woah-ah-oh

She's not a saint and she's not what you think
She's an actress, woah (She deserved it)
She's better known for the things that she does
On the mattress, woah
Soon, she's gonna find stealing other people's toys
On the playground won't make you many friends
She should keep in mind, she should keep in mind
There is nothing I do better than revenge

Do you still feel like you know what you're doing?
'Cause I don't think you do, oh (No, no, no, no)
Do you still feel like you know what you're doing? (No)
I don't think you do, I don't think you do
Let's hear the applause (Come on, come on)
Come on, show me how much better you are
(So much better, yeah?)
See, you deserve some applause
'Cause you're so much better
She took him faster than you could say "Sabotage"

vocab.definition: the act of intentionally causing something to fail or be unsuccessful

vocab.context: Taylor uses 'sabotage' to describe the woman's swift and intentional manipulation of her relationship.

vocab.definition: to judge someone or something as being less capable or effective than they actually are

vocab.definition: the process of preparing or making something ready for use or action

vocab.definition: the state of being worthy of respect or admiration

vocab.definition: the quality of being refined, cultured, and elegant

vocab.definition: a fashion or style that is popular at a particular time

vocab.definition: a person who is considered to be mentally ill or unstable

songs.title: Innocent

I hope you remember

Your string of lights is still bright to me, oh
Who you are is not where you've been
You're still an innocent
It's okay, life is a tough crowd

Who you are is not what you did
You're still an innocent

Can't breathe whenever you're gone

songs.title: Last Kiss
songs.lyrics:
I still remember the look on your face
Lit through the darkness at 1:58
The words that you whispered for just us to know
You told me you loved me, so why did you go away?
Away

I do recall now, the smell of the rain
Fresh on the pavement, I ran off the plane
That July ninth, the beat of your heart
It jumps through your shirt, I can still feel your arms

But now I'll go
Sit on the floor wearing your clothes
All that I know is I don't know
How to be something you miss

I never thought we'd have a last kiss
I never imagined we'd end like this
Your name, forever the name on my lips

I do remember the swing of your step
The life of the party, you're showing off again
And I'd roll my eyes and then you'd pull me in
I'm not much for dancing, but for you, I did
Because I love your handshake, meeting my father
I love how you walk with your hands in your pockets
How you'd kiss me when I was in the middle of saying something
There's not a day I don't miss those rude interruptions

And I'll go
Sit on the floor wearing your clothes
All that I know is I don't know
How to be something you miss

I never thought we'd have a last kiss
I never imagined we'd end like this
Your name, forever the name on my lips, ooh

So I'll watch your life in pictures like I used to watch you sleep
And I feel you forget me like I used to feel you breathe
And I'll keep up with our old friends just to ask them how you are
Hope it's nice where you are

And I hope the sun shines and it's a beautiful day
And something reminds you you wish you had stayed
You can plan for a change in the weather and time
But I never planned on you changing your mind

So, I'll go
Sit on the floor wearing your clothes
All that I know is I don't know
How to be something you miss

I never thought we'd have a last kiss
I never imagined we'd end like this
Your name, forever the name on my lips

Just like our last kiss
Forever the name on my lips
Forever the name on my lips
Just like our last

vocab.definition: causing a strong feeling or excitement; bright or radiant

vocab.context: Taylor uses 'lit' to describe the intense feeling of being with the person, even in the midst of darkness.

vocab.definition: lasting or seeming to last for an indefinitely long time

vocab.definition: lacking politeness or good manners

vocab.definition: something that breaks the continuity of a process or activity

vocab.definition: a series of images or photographs

vocab.definition: to breathe in and out, or to live or exist

vocab.definition: to make something different or to become different

songs.title: Long Live
songs.lyrics:
I said, "Remember this moment"
In the back of my mind
The time we stood with our shaking hands
The crowds in stands went wild
We were the kings and the queens
And they read off our names
The night you danced like you knew our lives
Would never be the same
You held your head like a hero
On a history book page
It was the end of a decade
But the start of an age

Long live the walls we crashed through
How the kingdom lights shined just for me and you
I was screaming, "Long live all the magic we made"
And bring on all the pretenders
One day, we will be remembered

I said, "Remember this feeling"
I pass the pictures around
Of all the years that we stood there on the sidelines
Wishing for right now
We are the kings and the queens
You traded your baseball cap for a crown
When they gave us our trophies
And we held them up for our town
And the cynics were outraged
Screaming, "This is absurd"
'Cause for a moment, a band of thieves
In ripped up jeans got to rule the world

Long live the walls we crashed through
How the kingdom lights shined just for me and you
I was screaming, "Long live all the magic we made"
And bring on all the pretenders, I'm not afraid
Long live all the mountains we moved
I had the time of my life fighting dragons with you
I was screaming, "Long live that look on your face"
And bring on all the pretenders
One day, we will be remembered

Hold on to spinning around
Confetti falls to the ground
May these memories break our fall
Will you take a moment? Promise me this
That you'll stand by me forever
But if, God forbid, fate should step in
And force us into a goodbye
If you have children some day
When they point to the pictures
Please, tell 'em my name
Tell 'em how the crowds went wild
Tell 'em how I hope they shine
Long live the walls we crashed through
I had the time of my life with you

Long, long live the walls we crashed through
How the kingdom lights shined just for me and you
And I was screaming, "Long live all the magic we made"
And bring on all the pretenders, I'm not afraid
Singing, "Long live all the mountains we moved"
I had the time of my life fighting dragons with you
And long, long live that look on your face
And bring on all the pretenders
One day, we will be remembered

vocab.definition: A period of 10 years, used to describe a specific time period.
vocab.lyric_snippet: It was the end of a decade
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to mark the end of a significant time period in her life.

vocab.definition: People who pretend to be something they're not, often used to describe people who are fake or insincere.
vocab.lyric_snippet: And bring on all the pretenders
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe people who are fake or insincere, and she's not afraid of them.

vocab.definition: People who are skeptical or distrustful, often used to describe people who are negative or pessimistic.
vocab.lyric_snippet: And the cynics were outraged
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe people who are negative or pessimistic, and they're outraged by her success.

vocab.definition: Something that is unreasonable or illogical, often used to describe something that is ridiculous or nonsensical.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Screaming, 'This is absurd'
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the reaction of the cynics, who think her success is unreasonable or illogical.

vocab.definition: People who steal or take something that doesn't belong to them, often used to describe people who are rebellious or nonconformist.
vocab.lyric_snippet: A band of thieves in ripped up jeans got to rule the world
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe herself and her friends, who are rebellious and nonconformist.

vocab.definition: Large natural elevations of the earth's surface, often used to describe challenges or obstacles.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Long live all the mountains we moved
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the challenges or obstacles that she and her friends have overcome.

vocab.definition: Mythical creatures that are often depicted as fire-breathing, scaly monsters, often used to describe challenges or obstacles.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I had the time of my life fighting dragons with you
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the challenges or obstacles that she and her friends have overcome, and she's had the time of her life doing it.

vocab.definition: A symbol of royalty or power, often used to describe something that is prestigious or esteemed.
vocab.lyric_snippet: You traded your baseball cap for a crown
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the prestige or esteem that she and her friends have achieved.
